updates.tweet_text:
This market remains all about one level ~7425. It was the Failed Breakdown here after 9:30AM yesterday that got us long for the rally to 7472 target. Last eve #ES_F tested 7425 *again* and bounced.

Plan today: Just ride your runner. 7463, 7472, 7492, 7510=targets. 7425=support https://t.co/G52V8jfV5W
updates.notes: All mentioned prices (7425, 7463, 7472, 7492, 7510) are either in the morning plan or trade management/target references. 7463 is closest to unlisted but framed as existing target list, not newly named actionable level.

updates.tweet_text: Note: There is nothing to do here but ride runner - no change to newsletter entries. Readers know I do not trade low volatility chop. For me to even open my platform I need a volatility expansion/elevator down sell. If you engage chop, you are trading noise.
updates.notes: Pure trade management/commentary tweet. No prices named at all. Describes a no-trade condition (low volatility chop) with no actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text: @mainftr Yes quite often...usually volatility also dies mid day. Not always though. Most days we get at least one good volatility expansion typically in then morning. Then I make my trade and shut down the platform. Can't trade low volatility
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about trading habits and volatility patterns. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
Nice pop to 7463 1st target in #ES_F but this is pure chop now. I don't trade chop. To open my platform, I need an elevator down sell and volatility expansion. Last trade was the 930AM 7427 Failed Breakdown ysd morning, which ran +70

7463, 7472, 7492, 7510 next. 7425=support https://t.co/bGTnEbd47x

updates.tweet_text: @CallingForPizza Hopefully can get it today! Nice little bear flag here so fingers crossed it breaks down and we get some red, which means trades

updates.tweet_text: @bigwood340 We don't predict regardless - just stay long, ride the runner, and if we break down then we get a new long opportunity

updates.tweet_text: @jwmolenaar In theory yes, but again don't want to predict ever. Just need to react to triggers. Price will always take the most complex path that will cause most retail traders to lose so if you predict you are creating biases that will stop you from trading the action live
updates.notes: Pure trading philosophy/methodology commentary. No price levels named, no actionable triggers or watchpoints mentioned at all.

updates.tweet_text: @jwmolenaar We could breakdown here, selloff 75 points, return to 7530, selloff for the rest of the year. Or we could rip to 7530 direct, dip, then clear it. Won't be a predictable path.
updates.notes: 7530 is already in the morning plan; the tweet is speculative commentary on possible paths, not naming any actionable new level.

updates.tweet_text: @DCdude69 7530 has to clear. It will allow ES to build a larger consolidation above it
updates.notes: 7530 is already in the morning plan; this is a reaffirmation/commentary on existing level, not a new actionable price.

updates.tweet_text: @sckdds In my experience probably 95% of the time. Very rare they don't backtest. This one should backtest too
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about backtest probability; no price named, no actionable level, no naming language.

updates.tweet_text:

updates.tweet_text: @hamburglar747 There's no timeline on it as I've spoken about at length. The June 5th breakdown took about 10 days to backtest. Typically does within a couple weeks!
updates.notes: Reply tweet discussing timeline of a breakdown backtest — no price named, no actionable level, pure conversational commentary.

updates.tweet_text: @hamburglar747 There are some that have taken 3-4 months but then on the other extreme we get some backtests within 3-4 days of the breakdown
updates.notes: Conversational reply about timeframes for backtests; no actionable price levels named whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text: @hamburglar747 Its not supposed to have any value for people trading, its just a magnet. For trading I trade Failed Breakdowns and manage it level to level. When 7530 backtests it could be an actionable backtest short for those who take those, but I don't
updates.notes: 7530 is a morning plan level; mention is conversational/conditional context, not a new actionable level introduction.

updates.tweet_text: @hamburglar747 As I repeat 4-5x everyday (look through my comments) we do not predict price action or price paths.
updates.notes: No price levels mentioned at all; tweet is a reply about methodology, purely conversational with zero actionable prices.

updates.tweet_text: @hamburglar747 How would I know that? I'm a trader not a psychic. I react to triggers
updates.notes: Reply to user explaining trading philosophy; no price levels named, no actionable triggers or levels of any kind.
